goal: Merge accepted cxs session-sources workflow into main

summary.title: Merge session-sources workflow into main
summary.what: Merged the accepted codex/session-sources-workflow branch into main, bringing in the multi-source architecture foundation while keeping Codex as the only public source and Claude Code reserved for later work.
summary.why: The user accepted the workflow and asked to merge it so the main checkout can carry the source adapter boundary, source-aware storage/query behavior, CLI --source surface, and updated usage docs without publishing a Claude Code adapter yet.
summary.user_goal: Merge accepted cxs session-sources workflow into main

decisions.point: How to resolve the src/query/find.ts merge conflict
decisions.chose: Preserve the source-aware read database guard and source_id filtering from the workflow branch while retaining main’s scannedMessageCount behavior.
decisions.rationale: This keeps old-index protection and multi-source isolation intact while preserving the already accepted reporting contract from main.
rejected: Drop withSourceAwareReadDb and allow source-unaware reads against upgraded query paths
Count scanned messages across all sources when no selector is provided

decisions.point: What source surface is public after the merge
decisions.chose: Expose Codex as the public source and keep claude-code registered only as a reserved, unsupported id.
decisions.rationale: The goal was to extract Codex assumptions and leave a clear Claude Code adapter boundary, not to publish Claude search behavior before its format/filtering/linearization policy is settled.
rejected: Ship a partial Claude Code adapter now
Add new commands for sources instead of a shared --source option

decisions.point: How to update local developer ergonomics after merge
decisions.chose: Switch the global dev cxs shim and both cxs skill symlinks back to the merged main checkout with scripts/cxs-switch.sh dev --repo /Users/envvar/work/repos/cxs.
decisions.rationale: The feature worktree is no longer the desired global dev target once the accepted branch is merged into main.
rejected: Leave global cxs pointing at the temporary session-sources worktree

rejected.alternative: Publish or enable Claude Code conversation search as part of this merge
rejected.reason: Explicitly out of scope; Claude Code format details and filtering/linearization boundaries remain future adapter work.

rejected.alternative: Change the fixed cxs command set
rejected.reason: The product boundary keeps status/sync/find/read-range/read-page/list/stats fixed; source selection is an option, not a new command surface.

review_notes: Conflict resolved in src/query/find.ts; source-aware read guard and per-source scannedMessageCount were preserved.
Post-merge verification passed: npm run check (27 test files, 172 tests).
Post-merge CLI smoke passed: npm --silent run cxs -- status --source codex --json exited 0 and reported indexVersion cxs-v7-source-identity.

Post-merge CLI smoke passed: npm --silent run cxs -- find needle --source codex --json --db docs/workflows/2026-06-05-session-sources/handoffs/E1-artifacts/e1-smoke.sqlite returned 1 result with scannedMessageCount 2.
Global dev cxs switched to /Users/envvar/work/repos/cxs; command -v cxs points at /Users/envvar/Library/pnpm/bin/cxs and the shim execs /Users/envvar/work/repos/cxs/dist/cli.js.
No push, PR, npm release, or release skill update was performed in this merge step.

architectural_claims: Session source is now an explicit adapter boundary instead of implicit Codex-only parser, inventory, and env coupling.
Codex is the only public adapter; claude-code remains a reserved id until a future adapter defines parsing, filtering, and linearization policy.
Selectors and coverage remain root-scoped while query/read paths enforce source-aware database access.
behavioral_changes: CLI commands accept --source <id> with public support for codex.
Unsupported claude-code selection is rejected instead of silently falling back to Codex behavior.
Source-aware upgraded indexes use cxs-v7-source-identity and old source-unaware indexes are guarded on read paths that require source identity.
Find scannedMessageCount is scoped to the selected source when no selector narrows the query.

api_changes.signature: --source <id> on status, sync, find, read-range, read-page, list, stats
query/read/store paths carry source identity and source-aware DB guards

data_model_changes.name: sessions source identity
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Indexes must be upgraded/synced to cxs-v7-source-identity before source-aware read commands can use them.

data_model_changes.name: source adapter registry
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Codex adapter is public; Claude Code is reserved for later implementation.

security_implications: Source filtering is enforced in SQL/query paths to avoid cross-source result leakage once multiple sources are added.
No new watcher, daemon, network integration, or credential handling was introduced.
migration_notes: Existing source-unaware indexes need sync/schema upgrade before source-aware read commands can operate normally.
status remains available as a read-only way to inspect context and schema state.
